[2018-03-27 18:50] http404error: @Masne98888 It's certainly a monster of your own doing, to some extent
[2018-03-27 18:50] http404error: Talk about something else
[2018-03-27 18:50] http404error: SSP already exhausted the 2-limit for today
[2018-03-27 18:51] http404error: So if you move the discussion topic, they're gone from memory
[2018-03-27 18:51] http404error: Also @Wilson I have to side against you with the Shalev's debate from earlier
[2018-03-27 18:51] http404error: I appreciate your argument that the situations where it would backfire are essentially academic
[2018-03-27 18:52] wilson0862 (Wilson): ya that's fair. I conceded that one.
[2018-03-27 18:52] http404error: But that's part of the definition - total domination in absolutely every case, no exceptions
[2018-03-27 18:52] http404error: It's entirely fair to say that is dumb and not worth preserving
[2018-03-27 18:53] http404error: And in the process of development Lunarch has consciously conceded some things that would otherwise dominate
[2018-03-27 18:53] http404error: In one's own designs it is often fair to argue that your unit has an upside that justifies a small break of some kind
[2018-03-27 18:53] http404error: But many times the conversion is very easy, and in that case it should be done
[2018-03-27 18:54] http404error: Anyways, sorry for the shpeal
[2018-03-27 18:54] wilson0862 (Wilson): I assume by conversion you mean to a non-rule breaking design?
[2018-03-27 18:54] wilson0862 (Wilson): Nah you're good. Appreciate your input. :D
[2018-03-27 18:54] http404error: Yes, such as converting an automated effect to a click effect, or so on
[2018-03-27 18:55] wilson0862 (Wilson): Yeah I can see that. I did it as a nerf and didn't think enough about it.
[2018-03-27 18:56] http404error: There are certainly cases where I feel a break would be justified though, and I have every confidence that Lunarch is sitting on designs that they feel the same way about
[2018-03-27 18:57] iminabearsuit: There's degrees of breaking though, I feel. Something that you don't want to kill on the opponent side, is a big nono i feel.
[2018-03-27 18:58] 307th: is arms race the only unit that (very slightly) breaks SDR currently in the game?
[2018-03-27 18:58] iminabearsuit: Where something with a downside on your side, is fine, if you were aware of it at the time of purchace.
[2018-03-27 18:58] 307th: cluster bolt too, i guess
[2018-03-27 18:58] iminabearsuit: I feel like a lot of new players have that relationship with chrono filter.
[2018-03-27 18:59] 307th: oh, and redeemer, i guess there's probably plenty of slight cases
[2018-03-27 18:59] iminabearsuit: Where you think "man I wish I didn't have this chrono filter, because my turns are so awkward". Its obviously better than no chrono filter. but it's a psychological thing.
[2018-03-27 19:03] http404error: Nothing in Prismata breaks SDR at all
[2018-03-27 19:03] http404error: That's trivially true though
[2018-03-27 19:04] http404error: Persistent downsides are bad to have, but only because it was decided so
[2018-03-27 19:05] http404error: The thing is that downsides can cause rippling effects
[2018-03-27 19:05] http404error: Currently since no unit has a persistent downside it can always be said that you would gladly accept any free unit at all
[2018-03-27 19:06] http404error: That doesn't have to be true, that part of SDR can be conceded if desired
[2018-03-27 19:06] 307th: i think there are (super-contrived) situations where buying arms race could lose you a game where you'd win if you didn't spend those resources on anything
[2018-03-27 19:06] iminabearsuit: Sometimes I don't wanna be seen winning with a savior in play.
[2018-03-27 19:06] 307th: i think that means it very slightly breaks SDR
[2018-03-27 19:06] http404error: @307th That's an interesting point
[2018-03-27 19:06] http404error: Let me think about that for a second
[2018-03-27 19:06] 307th: same would go for redeemer/cluster bolt
[2018-03-27 19:07] http404error: Buying a unit is never protected by SDR
[2018-03-27 19:07] iminabearsuit: I don't think buying is a part of SDR.
[2018-03-27 19:07] http404error: Nowhere is it said that buying a unit should dominate buying nothing (obviously floating can be good, but let's pretend you just never ever use the resources on anything else)
[2018-03-27 19:07] http404error: Spells introduce some weirdness for sure because they're not a single unit
[2018-03-27 19:08] 307th: oh, seems like i didn't remember the SDR article quite right then
[2018-03-27 19:09] iminabearsuit: You can say the same about Savior. Buying it is most of the time worse than not buying anything.
[2018-03-27 19:09] wilson0862 (Wilson): As far as I'm aware, SDR only applies to units that have already been bought.
[2018-03-27 19:09] wilson0862 (Wilson): Once they are bought, they should be strictly better than not having it.
[2018-03-27 19:10] http404error: Spells don't count as units - they only go on the field so you can cancel them
[2018-03-27 19:11] http404error: Otherwise, the little squares on the field are never bad to have
[2018-03-27 19:11] http404error: It *could have* been decided that "buying a unit or spell dominates throwing those resources away for nothing"
[2018-03-27 19:11] http404error: but all that does is disallow you from making redeemer and the various spells in the game
[2018-03-27 19:11] wilson0862 (Wilson): Right, but it's not necessary.

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: But that's part of the definition - total domination in absolutely every case, no exceptions

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: It's entirely fair to say that is dumb and not worth preserving

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: And in the process of development Lunarch has consciously conceded some things that would otherwise dominate

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: In one's own designs it is often fair to argue that your unit has an upside that justifies a small break of some kind

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: But many times the conversion is very easy, and in that case it should be done

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: Anyways, sorry for the shpeal

messages.author: wilson0862
messages.content: I assume by conversion you mean to a non-rule breaking design?

messages.author: wilson0862
messages.content: Nah you're good. Appreciate your input. :D

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: Yes, such as converting an automated effect to a click effect, or so on

messages.author: wilson0862
messages.content: Yeah I can see that. I did it as a nerf and didn't think enough about it.

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: There are certainly cases where I feel a break would be justified though, and I have every confidence that Lunarch is sitting on designs that they feel the same way about

messages.author: iminabearsuit
messages.content: There's degrees of breaking though, I feel. Something that you don't want to kill on the opponent side, is a big nono i feel.

messages.author: 307th
messages.content: is arms race the only unit that (very slightly) breaks SDR currently in the game?

messages.author: iminabearsuit
messages.content: Where something with a downside on your side, is fine, if you were aware of it at the time of purchace.

messages.author: 307th
messages.content: cluster bolt too, i guess

messages.author: iminabearsuit
messages.content: I feel like a lot of new players have that relationship with chrono filter.

messages.author: 307th
messages.content: oh, and redeemer, i guess there's probably plenty of slight cases

messages.author: iminabearsuit
messages.content: Where you think "man I wish I didn't have this chrono filter, because my turns are so awkward". Its obviously better than no chrono filter. but it's a psychological thing.

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: Nothing in Prismata breaks SDR at all

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: That's trivially true though

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: Persistent downsides are bad to have, but only because it was decided so

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: The thing is that downsides can cause rippling effects

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: Currently since no unit has a persistent downside it can always be said that you would gladly accept any free unit at all

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: That doesn't have to be true, that part of SDR can be conceded if desired

messages.author: 307th
messages.content: i think there are (super-contrived) situations where buying arms race could lose you a game where you'd win if you didn't spend those resources on anything

messages.author: iminabearsuit
messages.content: Sometimes I don't wanna be seen winning with a savior in play.

messages.author: 307th
messages.content: i think that means it very slightly breaks SDR

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: @307th That's an interesting point

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: Let me think about that for a second

messages.author: 307th
messages.content: same would go for redeemer/cluster bolt

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: Buying a unit is never protected by SDR

messages.author: iminabearsuit
messages.content: I don't think buying is a part of SDR.

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: Nowhere is it said that buying a unit should dominate buying nothing (obviously floating can be good, but let's pretend you just never ever use the resources on anything else)

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: Spells introduce some weirdness for sure because they're not a single unit

messages.author: 307th
messages.content: oh, seems like i didn't remember the SDR article quite right then

messages.author: iminabearsuit
messages.content: You can say the same about Savior. Buying it is most of the time worse than not buying anything.

messages.author: wilson0862
messages.content: As far as I'm aware, SDR only applies to units that have already been bought.

messages.author: wilson0862
messages.content: Once they are bought, they should be strictly better than not having it.

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: Spells don't count as units - they only go on the field so you can cancel them

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: Otherwise, the little squares on the field are never bad to have

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: It *could have* been decided that "buying a unit or spell dominates throwing those resources away for nothing"

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: but all that does is disallow you from making redeemer and the various spells in the game

messages.author: wilson0862
messages.content: Right, but it's not necessary.
